reasoning: The Lean formalization captures the same mathematical claim as the informal statement. AntitoneOn matches "nonstrictly decreasing" (non-strict version: a ≤ b → f b ≤ f a). The local conditions on [0, t] (DifferentiableOn, AntitoneOn on Icc 0 t) are essentially equivalent to global conditions, since any function satisfying these on [0, t] can be extended to ℝ preserving the global antitone second derivative (extend with constant x''(0) for τ < 0 and constant x''(t) for τ > t). The combined DifferentiableOn hypotheses on x and deriv x force continuity, making deriv x 0 = 0 and deriv x t = v correctly encode x'(0) = 0 and x'(t) = v. The solution 2s/v is achievable (by the quadratic x(τ) = (v²/(4s))τ²) and upper-bounds the set by concavity of x'. Both the informal and Lean sets have 2s/v as their greatest element, so IsGreatest captures "max is 2s/v" faithfully.
